issues.title: Invalid table reference
issues.problem: The query references a table 'users' which does not exist in the provided schema.
issues.fix: Replace 'users' with the correct table from the schema, e.g., 'sigma_analytics.fact_transactions'.

issues.title: Incorrect WHERE clause usage
issues.problem: The 'WHERE' clause is used incorrectly without specifying a table or condition related to the schema.
issues.fix: Remove the 'WHERE age > 21' clause and replace it with a valid condition from the schema.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic or purpose.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose and logic of the query.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT ft.transaction_id, ft.amount, ft.status, ft.merchant_id, ft.customer_id, ft.transaction_date, ft.payment_method, dm.merchant_name, dc.customer_name, dc.email, dc.tier, dc.signup_date, dc.city FROM sigma_analytics.fact_transactions ft JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant dm ON ft.merchant_id = dm.merchant_id JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_customer dc ON ft.customer_id = dc.customer_id WHERE ft.status = 'COMPLETED';
review.summary: The query had critical correctness issues due to invalid table references and an incorrect WHERE clause. Readability was also lacking due to the absence of comments.
